arraylisttester

Class ArrayList

  • java.lang.Object
    • arraylisttester.ArrayList

  • public class ArrayList
extends java.lang.Object
    A class that mimics the Java ArrayList. To demonstrate how the ArrayList in Java is actually written (or at least how it might be) to show that you can write your own data structure if you know what you want it to do and how to store the data.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      ArrayList()
      Constructs an empty list with an initial capacity of ten
      ArrayList(int capacity)
      Constructs an empty list with the specified initial capacity.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean add(int element)
      Appends the specified element to the end of the list
      boolean add(int index, int element)
      Inserts the specified element at the specified position in this list.
      void clear()
      Removes all of the element from this list.
      boolean contains(int element)
      does a linear search for the element and returns true if the element is present in the list or false otherwise
      int get(int index)
      Returns the value of the element at the specified index if the index is valid.
      int indexOf(int element)
      Finds the element specified and returns the index if found or -1 if not found.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Checks to see if there is anything in the list
      int remove(int index)
      if the index is valid it saves the element at that location and then moves all of the elements "down" on in the array and reduces the list size by one.
      int set(int index, int element)
      If the index is valid the current element is replaced with the value of the parameter.
      int[] toArray()
      Returns an array containing all of the elements in this list in proper sequence(from first to last)
      java.lang.String toString()
      Prints the list 20 items per line if there are elements in the list.
      void trimToSize()
      Trims the capacity of the ArrayLIst instance to be the list's current size

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • ArrayList

        public ArrayList()
        Constructs an empty list with an initial capacity of ten

      • ArrayList

        public ArrayList(int capacity)
        Constructs an empty list with the specified initial capacity.
        Parameters:
        capacity - the initial capacity of the arrayList

    • Method Detail

      • add

        public boolean add(int element)
        Appends the specified element to the end of the list
        Parameters:
        element - an element to be added
        Returns:
        true

      • add

        public boolean add(int index,
                   int element)
        Inserts the specified element at the specified position in this list.
        Parameters:
        index - the position in the list where the element should be added
        element - the element to be added
        Returns:
        true if the element is added and false otherwise.

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Removes all of the element from this list.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(int element)
        does a linear search for the element and returns true if the element is present in the list or false otherwise
        Parameters:
        element - the element we are looking for
        Returns:
        true if the element is in the list and false otherwise.

      • get

        public int get(int index)
        Returns the value of the element at the specified index if the index is valid.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the element that is to be returned
        Returns:
        the value of the element at the specified index or -1 if the index is invalid.

      • indexOf

        public int indexOf(int element)
        Finds the element specified and returns the index if found or -1 if not found. performs a standard linear search if the array contains the element.
        Parameters:
        element - the element to search for
        Returns:
        the index of the element or -1 if the element is in in the list

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Checks to see if there is anything in the list
        Returns:
        true if size is 0 and false otherwise

      • remove

        public int remove(int index)
        if the index is valid it saves the element at that location and then moves all of the elements "down" on in the array and reduces the list size by one.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the element to be removed
        Returns:
        the value of the element at the specified index.

      • set

        public int set(int index,
               int element)
        If the index is valid the current element is replaced with the value of the parameter.
        Parameters:
        index - the element location to be changed.
        element - the new value of the element at the specified location
        Returns:
        the value of the element that was replaced or -1 if the index is invalid

      • toArray

        public int[] toArray()
        Returns an array containing all of the elements in this list in proper sequence(from first to last)
        Returns:
        The underlying array

      • trimToSize

        public void trimToSize()
        Trims the capacity of the ArrayLIst instance to be the list's current size

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Prints the list 20 items per line if there are elements in the list. Prints empty list otherwise.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a string representing the contents of the list.
